*** 2 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALS EF-2 TORNADO DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM SOUTH SIDE OF MATTHEWS TO 1 MILE EAST OF TOWN. PATH LENGTH OF 1 MILE WITH MAX WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 120 MPH. DOZENS OF HOMES WITH ROOFS PARTIALLY OR COMPLETELY TORN OFF. SEMI TRUCKS WERE BLOWN OVER AND DESTROYED. NUMEROUS BUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. EXACT NUMBER OF INJURIES NOT KNOWN AT THIS TIME, BUT THERE WERE AT LEAST A FEW MINOR INJURIES DUE TO WINDOWS BEING BLOWN OUT OF REST HOME.
TWO WATER RESCUES WERE CONDUCTED ON FLOODED ROADS.
REPORTS OF WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES, CARS, AND AN OVERTURNED SEMI TRUCK IN MATTHEWS.
STRUCTURE AND WIND DAMAGE REPORTED NEAR MATTHEWS.
NUMEROUS SMALL LIMBS AND SMALL TREES DOWN IN TOWN. WIND ESTIMATED AT LEAST 60 MPH. LATE REPORT.
HIGH WATER RESCUE HAPPENING AT CHRIST ROAD AND KENTUCKY AVE.

UPDATED INFORMATION. EF1 TORNADO BEGAN SOUTH OF KREBS STATION RD AND ENDED NORTH OF CLARKS LINE RD. TORNADO DESTROYED A BARN. THREE HOMES AND A GARAGE WERE DAMAGED. PATH WIDTH 100 YARDS. PATH LENGTH 2 MILES. EYEWITNESS ACCOUNT OF TORNADO.

PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 100 MPH. EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM SOUTHEAST GRAVES INTO WEST CALLOWAY COUNTIES. PATH LENGTH 8.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH 125 YARDS. MORE THAN A HALF DOZEN BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. TWO EMPTY GRAIN BINS BLOWN OVER. SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED, MAINLY FROM FALLING TREES OR BLOWING DEBRIS. TWO SEMI TRAILERS OVERTURNED. TWO MOBILE HOMES BLOWN FROM FOUNDATIONS. DOZENS OF TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. ONE EYEWITNESS OBSERVED TORNADO.

BRIEF EF-0 TOUCHED DOWN AND UPROOTED A FEW PINE TREES. A FEW SHINGLES WERE OFF A HOUSE AND GARAGE. PEAK WINDS NEAR 65 MPH. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 0.15 MILE. PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. EYEWITNESS ACCOUNT OF TORNADO.

EF1 TORNADO.TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON BILL RILEY RD AND BLEW SOME SHINGLES OFF A ROOF AND UPROOTED OR SNAPPED TREES. TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND DAMAGED TREES ALONG THE WAY. ON BYRD RD THE ROOF OF A RESIDENCE WAS BRIEFLY UPLIFTED AND A WINDOW WAS BLOWN IN. BEHIND THE RESIDENCE A MOBILE HOME WITH A WOOD BASE WAS LIFTED/ROLLED ABOUT 30 YARDS DOWN A HILL. A SMALL OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED ON THE PROPERTY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST DAMAGING TREES AND LIFTED ALONG DAVE MILLER RD.
WATER OVER US HWY 61 NEAR MATTHEWS.

NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALS EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 105 MPH OCCURRED IN SOUTH PART OF MURRAY. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 1.5 MILES. MAX PATH WIDTH ABOUT 100 YARDS. AT LEAST 40 HOMES DAMAGED, PRIMARILY DUE TO SHINGLE LOSS. TWO HOMES WERE A TOTAL LOSS, WITH ROOF DECKING REMOVED, WINDOWS BLOWN OUT, AND PART OF AN EXTERIOR WALL CAVED IN DUE TO PROJECTILE IMPACTS. AT LEAST FOUR BUSINESSES DAMAGED. DOZENS OF TREES DOWNED.
POWER OUTAGES REPORTED IN MURRAY AND AT THE MURRAY STATE CAMPUS.
TREE INSIDE A HOME ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF BENTON.
TREE REPORTED DOWN BLOCKING BOTH LANES OF ROAD NEAR INTERSECTION OF REDDICK POND ROAD AND WILL JACKSON ROAD. TIME APPROXIMATED BY RADAR.
WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE, POWER OUTAGES, AND WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AT FOOD GIANT.
ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE TO A COMMERCIAL BUILDING ON THE EAST SIDE OF 12TH ST, SOUTH OF SYCAMORE ST.
TREES DOWN AND ROOF DAMAGE ON 16TH ST. IN MURRAY.

*** 9 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALS EF-2 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 135 MPH OCCURRED FROM THE SOUTH SIDE OF HOPKINSVILLE TO 5.5 MILES EAST OF HOPKINSVILLE. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 5.8 MILES AND PEAK WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. ALL OF THE INJURIES OCCURRED IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX. THREE APARTMENT BUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED WITH THE ROOF AND MOST WALLS BLOWN DOWN ON THE SECOND STORY. AT LEAST 4 RETAIL BUILDING SUSTAINED MAJOR ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE WITH WINDOWS AND DOORS BLOWN IN OR BROKEN.

EF-0 TORNADO TRACKED 5.5 MILES ACROSS LAKE MORRIS AREA. PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. PEAK WINDS 85 MPH. AT LEAST FOUR BARNS OR SHEDS WERE DAMAGED, PRIMARILY DUE TO PARTIAL LOSS OF ROOFING AND SIDING. ONE CAMPER TRAILER OVERTURNED. HUNDREDS OF TREES WITH BROKEN LIMBS. SOME UPROOTED TREES. ONE EYEWITNESS ACCOUNT OF A TORNADO.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AT EAGLE CROSSING APARTMENTS ON CALVIN DRIVE. REPORTS OF MULTIPLE NON-LIFE-THREATENING INJURIES. TIME APPROXIMATED BY RADAR.
NUMEROUS BUILDINGS WITH DAMAGE INCLUDING A KMART AND THE SECOND STORY OF AN APARTMENT COMPLEX, SEVERAL POWER OUTAGES IN THE HOPKINSVILLE AREA. TIME APPROXIMATED BY RADAR.

BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 189 ABOUT A MILE WEST OF THE TODD COUNTY LINE. PEAK WINDS 65 MPH. PATH LENGTH 0.5 MILE. PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. TWO GARAGES AND FARM BUILDINGS SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. A FEW TREE LIMBS BROKEN. ONE EYEWITNESS ACCOUNT OF TORNADO.

NUMEROUS BUILDINGS WITH DAMAGE INCLUDING A KMART AND THE SECOND STORY OF AN APARTMENT COMPLEX, SEVERAL POWER OUTAGES IN THE HOPKINSVILLE AREA.

STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH AND A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 2/3 MILE. SEVERAL HOMES HAD SHINGLE OR ROOF DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD SOFFIT DAMAGE ALONG WITH A SMALL SHED THAT WAS DESTROYED. SOME TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AS WELL.

EF-1 TORNADO, WITH PEAK WIND SPEED OF 105 MPH WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 10.25 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 125 YARDS. THE TORNADO PATH STARTED AT 517 PM CDT IN CARTERVILLE,IL, THEN LIFTED 1.25 MILES E OF WHITE ASH, IL AT 533 PM CDT. ONE TO TWO DOZEN HOMES RECEIVED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE. ONE BUSINESS IN ENERGY IL SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE WITH CONSIDERABLE ROOF LOSS AND BLOWN OUT WINDOWS. SEVERAL BARNS, SHEDS, AND OTHER STRUCTURES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. HUNDREDS OF TREES DAMAGED. POWER POLES SNAPPED.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE NORTH OF PITTSBURG, TRAVELLED 2 MILES AND LIFTED 2.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF PITTSBURG. PEAK WIND WAS 95 MPH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS. AT LEAST A HALF DOZEN HOMES WITH LOSS OF SHINGLES AND ONE HOME WITH WINDOWS BLOWN OUT. A COUPLE OF BARNS WITH PARTIAL ROOF LOSS. A FENCE WAS BLOWN OVER. DOZENS OF TREES WITH BROKEN LIMBS OR SNAPPED TRUNKS.

PATH STRETCHED FROM 7.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GALATIA IL AT 547 PM CDT TO 1.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF RALEIGH IL AT 607 PM CDT. THREE HOMES DESTROYED WITH OVER A DOZEN OTHERS DAMAGED WITH AT LEAST PARTIAL LOSS OF SHINGLES AND/OR SIDING, OR OTHER DAMAGE. DOZENS OF BARNSAND OTHER STRUCTURES DESTROYED OR HEAVILY DAMAGED. EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF PENERMON AND LIFTED 0.5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF BAKER. PATH LENGTH WAS 4.5 MILES WITH PEAK WINDS OF 85MPH. SEVERAL WINDOWS WERE BROKEN, WITH PARTIAL LOSS OF METAL ROOF COVERING AND THE TOP BROKEN OFF OF A CHIMNEY ON A HOME. TWO BARNS HAD PARTIAL LOSS OF METAL ROOFING AND WALLS BLOWN OFF. A GARAGE SUSTAINED LOSS OF SHINGLES AND HAD LARGE ENTRY DOOR BLOWN IN. SEVERAL TREES HAD LARGE LIMBS BROKEN OFF. SEVERAL WOODEN POWER POLES WERE BLOWN PART
...UNCOMFIRMED...TORNADO PERIODIC TOUCHDOWNS ON HIGHWAY 181 NEAR THE COMMUNTIY.

REPORT OF TORNADO AND DAMAGE TO HOME IN THE GALATIA AREA. ...TORNADO UNCONFIRMED...9 HOMES DAMAGED ALONG HARPER ROAD. 3 TO 4 HOMES DESTROYED. 2 BARNS/POLEBARNS DAMAGED. ROOF OFF OF HOUSE ALONG BANK LICK ROAD.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH A PEAK WIND OF 110 MPH WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 125 YARDS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT APPROX. 614 PM CDT, 3 MILES NW OF MATTHEWS AND LIFTED AT 617 PM CDT, 1 MILE NNW OF MATTHEWS. LARGE FARM IMPLEMENT BUILDING DESTROYED, PARTS OF BUILDING WERE LOFTED OVER 1 MILE AWAY. FOUR BARNS/GARAGES SUSTAINED DAMAGE.A GRAIN BIN WAS BLOWN IN ON ONE SIDE. ONE HOME HAD REAR PORCH ROOF BLOWN OFF,A BROKEN WINDOW,AND SHINGLES TORN OFF. WINDOW BROKEN AT ANOTHER HOME.
TWO STRUCTURES DOWN AS WELL AS POWER POLES ALONG CR820
TWO STRUCTURES DOWN AS WELL AS POWER POLES ALONG STATE HIGHWAY FF NEAR CR820

EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS AROUND 85 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 8 MILES, LIFTING ROUGHLY 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF METROPOLIS NEAR LOVERKAMP ROAD. NUMEROUS BUILDINGS WITH SHINGLE DAMAGE WERE SEEN AS WELL AS LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN WITH BRADFORD PEAR TREES SNAPPED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. A SEMI TRAILER WAS TIPPED OVER AND SEVERAL IRRIGATION PIPES WERE BENT IN ONE 20 ACRE FIELD. DAMAGE PATH WAS 50 YARDS LONG. DEBRIS THROWN 200-400 YARDS.

CONFIRMED EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 110 MPH. TORNADO BEGAN AT 638 PM CDT 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF NEW HAVEN IN WHITE COUNTY, IL, AND ENDED AT 642 PM CDT 7.25 MILES NORTHEAST OF NEW HAVEN IN WHITE COUNTY, IL. PATH LENGTH WAS 2.25 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 225 YARDS. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES. THE TORNADO DAMAGED AND MOVED FARMING IRRIGATION EQUIPMENT. IRRIGATION PIVOT SYSTEM AND A SEMI TANK TRAILER OVERTURNED. THREE BARNS LOST METAL ROOFING. DOZENS OF LARGE TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES WEST OF BURNA TRAVELLED 4.3 MILES AND LIFTED 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF BURNA. THE PEAK WIND WAS 125 MPH AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. ONE WELL-BUILT TWO STORY HOME WAS TWISTED ON ITS BASEMENT FOUNDATION. ALL BASEMENT WALLS WERE CRACKED AND WATER LINES WERE BROKEN. DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. FOUR VEHICLES WERE DESTROYED. ONE WAS ROLLED 50 FEET. A FEW OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED.
TORNADO REPORTED NEAR THE COMMUNITY RELAYED THROUGH TRAINED SPOTTER.
TIME UNKNOWN...399 CUSTOMERS LOST POWER THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 1/3 MILE AND MAX WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, A ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF A BARN, A METAL ROOF WAS PEELED BACK ON A HOUSE, AND A ROTTEN TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME AND DESTROYED IT.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 1/3 MILE AND MAX WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, A ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF A BARN, A METAL ROOF WAS PEELED BACK ON A HOUSE, AND A ROTTEN TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME AND DESTROYED IT.
TREE ON MOBILE HOME AT INTERSECTION OF STATE HIGHWAY 1540 AND 58 WEST. NO INJURIES REPORTED. RESCUE SQUAD RESPONDED.
EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY A TENTH OF A MILE AND MAX WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS. ALL WALLS WERE BLOWN OUT OF A CHICKEN BARN.

CONFIRMED EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH. TORANDO BEGAN AT 736 PM JUST SOUTH OF KY HIGHWAY 1748 W AND DAMAGED TREES, SOME UPROOTED. IT MOVED NORTHEAST TO MACEDONIA CHURCH RD WHERE TIN FROM A BARN ROOF WAS LOFTED INTO A NEARBY FIELD. AN UNOCCUPIED MOBILE HOME WAS MOVED ABOUT 4 FEET OFF ITS FOUNDATION. THE WALLS WERE GONE BUT THE WOOD FRAME AND ROOF REMAINED. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.75 MILES. PATH WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS. TORNADO LIFTED AT 739 PM 4.24 MILES NNE OF WINGO.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 5 MILES EAST OF FRUITLAND AND LIFTED 8 MILES EAST OF FRUITLAND NEAR TRAIL OF TEARS STATE PARK. PATH LENGTH WAS 2.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. DOZENS OF TREES, SOME LARGE, WERE BROKEN OR UPROOTED. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED FROM FALLEN TREES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS UP TO 95 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF WOLF LAKE AND LIFTED 1 MILE EAST OF WOLF LAKE. PATH LENGTH WAS 3.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. A VERY LARGE EQUIPMENT BARN COLLAPSED AND ANOTHER BARN WAS DESTROYED. A ONE STORY HOUSE WAS PARTIALLY SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION. ALL STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS ALONG THE WARE WOLF LAKE ROAD.

A MICROBURST WITH PEAK ESTIMATED WINDS OF 90 MPH OCCURRED ALONG A 25 MILE PATH FROM NW POSEY COUNTY INTO SOUTHERN GIBSON COUNTY. *** 1 INJ *** DAMAGE AREA IS ON NORTH SIDE OF TOWN BETWEEN 8TH STREET AND BRAVARD PLACE. 45 HOMES IMPACTED AND 6 STREETS IMPASSABLE. ONE PERSON TAKEN TO HOSPITAL. NUMEROUS STREETS FLOODED IN MOUNT CARMEL. REPORT RELAYED VIA TWITTER.
NUMEROUS ROADS FLOODED ACROSS THE COUNTY.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 110 MPH STRUCK NEAR STANLEY, KY. THE TORNADO BEGAN 1.2 MILES SW OF STANLEY AND ENDED 1.3 MILES E OF STANLEY. THE ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH WAS 2.25 MILES. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. ONE HOME HAS ITS EAST WALL NEARLY COMPLETELY BLOWN OUT. A FEW HOMES HAD STEEL ROOFS TORN AWAY. SEVERAL HOMES HAD SIDING OR FACIA DAMAGE. THE TORNADO BLEW DOWN CORN AND TOBACCO CROPS ALONG ITS PATH. DOZENS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR HAD TOPS BROKEN.
TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES JUST SOUTH OF STANLEY. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
TORNADO DAMAGE REPORTED IN TOWN. METAL BARN ROOF DESTORYED. SEVERAL TREE TOPS SNAPPED OFF. DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG HIGHWAY 60. TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES JUST SOUTH OF STANLEY.
EF0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED 80 MPH WINDS TOUCHED DOWN ON HIGHWAY 279 AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 0.6 MILES TO HIGHWAY 56. VIDEO OF TORNADO,DRONE FOOTAGE FROM PARTNERS, AND RADAR DATA USED.
EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN .9 MILES SOUTHEAST OF MACEO. A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED AND THERE WAS MINOR SOFFIT DAMAGE TO A HOME. PATH LENGTH .5 MILES AND A MAX WIND SPEED OF 95 MPH.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH. THE DAMAGE STARTED AT 11:52 AM CST 5 MILES SW OF SYMSONIA, KY, AND ENDED AT 11:53 AM CST 4.5 MILES W OF SYMSONIA, KY. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 1 MILE IN LENGTH WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 0.4 MILES. AT LEAST THREE BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED, INCLUDING THE ROOF STRUCTURE BEING LIFTED OFF A CARPORT. SEVERAL TREES OR TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A BARN ON KY 994 ACROSS FROM THE NORTH GRAVES FIRE DEPARTMENT.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST OFFSHORE IN BARKLEY LAKE NEAR BLUE WATER ESTATES. AT LEAST SIX HOMES DAMAGED...MAINLY LOSS OF SHINGLES AND VINYL SIDING. THERE WERE SOME BROKEN WINDOWS AND SCREENS BLOWN IN. THREE GARAGE DOORS WERE BLOWN INWARD AND ONE GARAGE HAD LOSS OF SHINGLES. A COUPLE OF DOZEN TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THERE WERE TWO SEPARATE EYE WITNESS REPORTS OF A TORNADO.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN SOUTHWEST INDIANA IN SPENCER COUNTY INDIANA. IT DAMAGED AT LEAST THREE HOMES. MOSTLY ROOF AND FACIA DAMAGE. ONE GARAGE DOOR AND WINDOW BLOWN IN. A DETACHED GARAGE WAS DESTROYED WITH THE DEBRIS THROWN AT LEAST 300 YARDS.AN OUTDOOR STORAGE SHED WAS PUSHED SEVERAL FEET OFF ITS FOUNDATION.MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO AT LEAST THREE METAL FARM BUILDINGS. AT LEAST TWO DOZEN TREES AND LIMBS WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 105 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2.4 MILES ENE OF MARIAH HILL AND LIFTED 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF FERDINAND. PATH LENGTH WAS 2 MILES WITH MAX WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. AT LEAST 3 HOMES WERE DAMAGED, WITH MOSTLY ROOF AND FACIA DAMAGE. SCATTERED STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED JUST SOUTH OF THE TORNADO PATH.

PRELIMINARY REPORT. SHED/OUTBUILDING COMPLETELY TORN APART/DESTROYED. SMALL YARDBARN DESTROYED. ROOFING/SHINGLES MISSING FROM HOUSE, FLASHING OFF OF GARAGE AND UNDER PORCH. NEIGHBOR WITH PART OF ROOF TORN OFF OF HOME. DELAYED REPORT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
